text: A realistic checkout change can look complete in one file and still miss the work that makes it correct. workspace dot json gives Codex local repository evidence before that risky edit lands.
displayText: A realistic checkout change can look complete in one file and still miss the work that makes it correct. workspace.json gives Codex local repository evidence before that risky edit lands.
visual: Product lockup, repository, and checkout task

text: Here, Codex receives a bounded request to change checkout behavior. An unprotected proposal can be locally plausible: update the route, see a clean-looking diff, and stop. But a checkout-only change can omit the session and formatting partners that the repository has previously needed. This is a controlled experimental fixture, not a production incident.
displayText: Here, Codex receives a bounded request to change checkout behavior. An unprotected proposal can be locally plausible: update the route, see a clean-looking diff, and stop. But a checkout-only change can omit the session and formatting partners that the repository has previously needed. This is a controlled experimental fixture, not a production incident.
visual: Baseline checkout-only proposal; label it synthetic and incomplete

text: Before the supported edit, workspace dot json surfaces the relevant history and evidenced co-change partners. Its deterministic hook evaluates the proposed changeset, not a model hunch. When the route is present but required partners are absent, the hook denies the incomplete edit and explains the recorded basis. That denial is a prompt to investigate, not a claim that every checkout change needs the same files.
displayText: Before the supported edit, workspace.json surfaces the relevant history and evidenced co-change partners. Its deterministic hook evaluates the proposed changeset, not a model hunch. When the route is present but required partners are absent, the hook denies the incomplete edit and explains the recorded basis. That denial is a prompt to investigate, not a claim that every checkout change needs the same files.
visual: Evidence panel followed by deterministic denial; reserve silence after denial

text: Codex then revises the changeset to cover the route and its evidenced partners. The story is not that the tool writes the change. The story is that repository context changes the decision before the edit. Targeted verification then runs against the corrected fixture. Exact command, commit, and result: bracketed HAC-98 placeholder pending preserved evidence.
displayText: Codex then revises the changeset to cover the route and its evidenced partners. The story is not that the tool writes the change. The story is that repository context changes the decision before the edit. Targeted verification then runs against the corrected fixture. [HAC-98: insert exact command, commit, and result after evidence freeze.]
visual: Corrected multi-file diff and targeted test; reserve silence after pass

text: After the completed change, a read-only G P T five point six reviewer examines the proposal and its evidence. It can challenge reasoning, name gaps, and leave a visible advisory verdict. It cannot edit files, and it cannot alter the hook decision. Reviewer outcome: bracketed HAC-136 placeholder pending the captured artifact. A pass is not a safety certification.
displayText: After the completed change, a read-only GPT-5.6 reviewer examines the proposal and its evidence. It can challenge reasoning, name gaps, and leave a visible advisory verdict. It cannot edit files, and it cannot alter the hook decision. [HAC-136: insert captured reviewer outcome.] A pass is not a safety certification.
visual: Visible GPT-5.6 reviewer artifact; reserve silence after verdict

text: That separation is deliberate. Evidence is the local M C P context plane. Deterministic enforcement is the action plane. The reviewer is the challenge plane. Installation is local, managed, and reversible: the package adds only marked configuration and removes only what it owns. It works over standard I O, keeps repository contents local at runtime, and does not replace tests or human review.
displayText: That separation is deliberate. Evidence is the local MCP context plane. Deterministic enforcement is the action plane. The reviewer is the challenge plane. Installation is local, managed, and reversible: the package adds only marked configuration and removes only what it owns. It works over stdio, keeps repository contents local at runtime, and does not replace tests or human review.
visual: Three-plane architecture and managed install/uninstall path

text: The boundary is simple. Missing evidence is unknown, not safe. The hook can justify a block or warning; it never certifies an edit as safe. The reviewer remains advisory. Human authority remains final. workspace dot json helps Codex make a better informed decision, while the repository and its maintainers keep control.
displayText: The boundary is simple. Missing evidence is unknown, not safe. The hook can justify a block or warning; it never certifies an edit as safe. The reviewer remains advisory. Human authority remains final. workspace.json helps Codex make a better informed decision, while the repository and its maintainers keep control.
visual: Trust boundary, fixture disclosure, and final lockup
